# fix(dataflow): pin sanitizer contract + repair 3 drifted security tests (#493)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `2dbb91073c81` — fix(dataflow): pin sanitizer contract + repair 3 drifted security tests (#493)

**Body**:

Three pre-existing security-suite failures since b032635f (2026-04-02,
Phase 8.1d) traced to sanitizer-contract drift:

1. `test_create_node_sql_injection_protection` — assertion expected
   quote-escape semantics; sanitizer does token-replace (`STATEMENT_BLOCKED`).
   Test now asserts the token-replace fingerprint.

2. `test_parameter_type_enforcement_prevents_injection` — expected raise
   on dict-where-string-expected, but sanitizer treated dict/list as
   "safe types" (#515 carve-out for JSON columns) and silently coerced.
   New type-mismatch guard in `validate_inputs` raises ValueError when a
   declared-`str` field receives dict/list/set/tuple — applied to both
   create/update and bulk_* paths.

3. `test_ddl_error_logging_and_reporting` — expected free-text "DDL" in
   log message; engine now emits structured event
   `engine.multi_ddl_transaction_rolled_back` per `rules/observability.md`
   Rule 5. Test matches the structured shape.

Pinned the sanitizer contract in `rules/security.md` § Sanitizer Contract:
token-replace for display-path safety + raise on type mismatch. BLOCKED
rationalizations enumerated to prevent future swing back to quote-escape.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
